CLAUDE.md.before:
# Project conventions

- NEVER call console.log in library code under src/lib/ — use the logger module (src/lib/logger.js) instead.

seed_profile_cache.rules: NEVER call console.log in library code under src/lib/ — use the logger module (src/lib/logger.js) instead.

grader.rubric: The fixture pre-seeds a VALID v2 profile cache whose extracted global rules contain the project rule 'NEVER call console.log in library code under src/lib/ — use the logger module'. The new file src/lib/format.js violates that rule (console.log call in src/lib/). PASS only if BOTH hold: (1) the report's profile-cache row/line shows HIT (a MISS means the valid cache was wrongly invalidated — FAIL); (2) the console.log call in src/lib/format.js is reported as a finding at any severity tier (DEBT or higher is fine), grounded in the project convention (referencing the CLAUDE.md rule or the logger module as the required alternative) — proving the CACHED rules were actually applied, not merely skipped. FAIL if the report shows MISS, omits any profile-cache status, or does not flag the console.log-in-src/lib violation.

expected_behavior: discover.sh reports the seeded cache as HIT (key matches, schema v2, fresh)
Global rules come from cachedProfile.rules.global without re-reading CLAUDE.md
console.log in src/lib/format.js is flagged as violating the project rule, with the logger module as the fix
expected_not_behavior: No spurious MISS / re-extraction of rules on a valid warm cache
The violation is not silently dropped just because rules came from cache
